description_long: This object is a historical artillery cannon mounted on a wooden wheeled carriage. The cannon barrel is long, cylindrical, and made of aged, patinated metal, likely bronze or iron, with decorative rings and a flared muzzle. The carriage is constructed from heavy wooden planks, reinforced with metal brackets and featuring four solid wooden wheels for mobility. Metal fastenings and bolts are visible along the carriage, and two lifting rings are mounted atop the barrel for handling. The overall appearance is robust and utilitarian, with weathered textures and a greenish-brown color palette. This type of cannon would have been used in historical urban fortifications or during sieges.
description: A weathered, long-barreled metal cannon mounted on a sturdy wooden wheeled carriage, featuring metal reinforcements and lifting rings. The object has a greenish patina and a robust, historical appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular muzzle of the cannon barrel and the symmetrical arrangement of the wooden wheels and carriage.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the cannon barrel, the wooden carriage, and the four wheels, with visible metal fastenings and lifting rings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back end of the cannon barrel, the carriage's rear structure, and the alignment of the wheels.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring view 1, showing the length of the barrel, carriage details, and wheel arrangement.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One fixed cannon barrel mounted centrally on the wooden carriage.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: None visible; surface is a solid, weathered green and brown patina.
